refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by ensuring timely data acquisition, processing, and dissemination.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into sub-functions addressing data acquisition, processing, and dissemination, which are key contributors to latency.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data acquisition start to worksite model update completion under representative operational scenarios and verify p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: Acquire real-time telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within required latency
Process and integrate telemetry data to update the worksite model within latency constraints
Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules and stakeholders within latency constraints

candidate_dps: Implement low-latency telemetry data acquisition subsystem
Use optimized data processing algorithms with parallelization
Deploy efficient dissemination protocols over network links
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement low-latency telemetry data acquisition subsystem; Use optimized data processing algorithms with parallelization; Deploy efficient dissemination protocols over network links

potential_sources: Telemetry data acquisition tightly coupled with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Data processing algorithms require close integration with NGHE Solution module
Dissemination protocols depend on Network Link interface
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically cohesive and map well to distinct functional subdomains, minimizing cross-dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link bandwidth and reliability meet latency requirements
Risk of processing bottlenecks if data volume spikes unexpectedly
Assumes telemetry data sources provide timely and accurate data
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor processing load and network performance; consider fallback modes if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for data processing and worksite model update logic

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Disseminates updated worksite model to external stakeholders and modules

module_containment_scores.notes: Core processing and update logic contained here; minimal diffusion.
Provides telemetry data but does not process updates.
Telemetry source and tasking interface; limited containment.
Not involved in update processing; only receives alerts.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update processing in NGHE Solution minimizes latency and diffusion, enabling tight control over timing.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module is overloaded
Dependency on timely telemetry input from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider load balancing or modularizing processing within NGHE Solution if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well decomposed and the selected DPs logically address each sub-FR with clear mapping.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists due to telemetry data crossing module boundaries
Processing concentrated in NGHE Solution may create bottlenecks
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the primary owner of update logic, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end using telemetry and network monitoring tools.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of sub-functions to NGHE Solution and interfaces to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

recommended_revisions: Monitor processing load in NGHE Solution for potential bottlenecks
Validate telemetry data timeliness and accuracy from Vehicle and Teleoperation

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the latency requirement with clear ownership and manageable risks. Verification approach is feasible.
next_level_sub_frs: Acquire real-time telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within required latency
Process and integrate telemetry data to update the worksite model within latency constraints
Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules and stakeholders within latency constraints

revise.why: Current level is sufficient; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link bandwidth and reliability are sufficient
Telemetry data sources provide timely and accurate data
open_risks: Processing bottlenecks under high data volume
Telemetry data delays impacting update latency
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under representative operational scenarios
Telemetry data timeliness and accuracy validation
